problem_title: Audit readiness weakens across scattered records
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to prepare AML evidence when alert data, provider outputs and case notes remain dispersed across tools. This fragmentation delays internal audits and complicates external regulatory reviews, increasing manual workload and risk of incomplete documentation.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates alerts, provider outputs, reviewer notes and evidence into a single workflow so compliance teams can efficiently prepare audit-ready AML records and supporting documentation.
workflow_description: SFTY routes compliance alerts through structured reviews, case creation, decision recording and evidence preservation to prepare audit-ready AML documentation.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects outputs from multiple compliance providers into structured cases, decisions and evidence supporting clear audit trails and internal or regulatory AML reviews.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASP compliance teams by preserving structured evidence and enabling controlled workflows for due diligence, provider orchestration and enterprise-grade deployment.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow

authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review

faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams a connected workflow layer connecting onboarding, KYT, Travel Rule, screening, case management and reporting for clearer, auditable and scalable AML operations.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle AML audit preparation?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures AML records, alerts, cases and decisions into audit-ready packages that support internal and external AML review workflows for VASPs and regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready AML evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves comprehensive decision logs, evidence packages and case histories with linked provider data to support thorough audit trails and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing KYC, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers, connecting their outputs into one compliance operating system without requiring provider replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace KYC, KYT or Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC, KYT, Travel Rule or screening providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, decisions and reporting for better AML compliance control.
final_description: Review how SFTY structures AML case management, evidence packages and regulatory rule updates for internal audit and regulatory review.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
